dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9351 IH: Continuity of Military Parents’ Academic Schooling and State Standards Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Harrigan introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to provide relief for members of the uniformed services who homeschool their dependent children, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Continuity of Military Parents’ Academic Schooling and State Standards Act of 2026 or the COMPASS Act.
The purposes of this Act are—

paragraph.text: to ensure uninterrupted educational continuity for military-connected students;
to reduce administrative and legal burdens caused by frequent relocations;
to prevent conflicting State homeschooling requirements from penalizing military families;
to support family stability and readiness of servicemembers (as defined in section 101 of the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. 3911)) during transitions; and
to align homeschooling treatment more closely with existing protections tied to domicile and legal residence afforded to military families under Federal law.

section.header: Treatment of compulsory education and homeschooling laws of States under Servicemembers Civil Relief Act

subsection.text: Title VII of the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. 4021 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following new section:

section.header: Compulsory education and homeschooling laws of States

subsection.text: A dependent child of a servicemember who is educated at home and is covered by military orders for a permanent change of station to a location shall be considered in compliance with all compulsory education and homeschooling provisions of law of the State of the location, if such dependent child is in compliance with all the compulsory education and homeschooling provisions of law of such State or the State of the legal residence of the servicemember.
In this section, the term child has the meaning given such term in section 101 of title 38, United States Code.
The table of contents in section 1(b) of such Act is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 707 the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 708. Compulsory education and homeschooling laws of States.
